label: Robberies & Raids
title: Loud scores with fast windows and faster consequences.
description: These jobs put crews into direct contact with civilians, alarms, and police pressure. They are ideal when you want public scenes, visible tension, and short decisions that matter.

label: Heists & Contracts
title: Organised crime starts with planning and ends with fallout.
description: This lane is for bigger crews, longer prep, and specialist roles. It is where criminal RP feels most like an operation instead of a random cash grab.

label: Vehicle Crime & Fraud
title: Steal, disguise, strip, and repurpose what moves.
description: Vehicle-focused crime adds garages, mechanics, scouting, and prep work to the underworld. It suits characters who prefer technical jobs and quieter setup scenes before the chaos starts.

label: Street Hustles & Disorder
title: Low-profile money, public nuisance, and fast opportunism.
description: These activities keep characters active between bigger scores and help neighbourhood reputation grow. They work best when players want repeatable pressure, territory presence, and everyday criminal texture.

label: Black Market & Utility
title: Prepare the tools and steal the resources bigger jobs rely on.
description: This lane feeds the wider underworld through crafting, supplies, and dirty logistics. It is ideal for characters who want to enable crime as much as commit it.
